author: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy
book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: God Alone Perfectly Good.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: What harm, you ask, would follow if those things too were perfectly good?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Still, should any one, who admits and believes the perfect goodness of God the Father, inquire what source we should reverently assign to any other perfectly good thing, supposing it to exist, our only correct reply would be, that it is of God the Father, who is perfectly good.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And we must bear in mind that what is of Him is born of Him, and not made by Him out of nothing, and that it is therefore perfectly, that is, incorruptibly, good like God Himself.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: So we see that it is unreasonable to require that things made out of nothing should be as perfectly good as He who was begotten of God Himself, and who is one as God is one, otherwise God would have begotten something unlike Himself.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Hence it shows ignorance and impiety to seek for brethren for this only-begotten Son through whom all good things were made by the Father out of nothing, except in this, that He condescended to appear as man.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Accordingly in Scripture He is called both only-begotten and first-begotten; only-begotten of the Father, and first-begotten from the dead.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: "And we beheld," says John, "His glory, the glory as of the only-begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth."

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And Paul says, "that He might be the first-born among many brethren."

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But should we say, These things made out of nothing are not good things, but only God’s nature is good, we shall be unjust to good things of great value.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And there is impiety in calling it a defect in anything not to be what God is, and in denying a thing to be good because it is inferior to God.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Pray submit then, thou nature of the rational soul, to be somewhat less than God, but only so far less, that after Him nothing else is above thee.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Submit, I say, and yield to Him, lest He drive thee still lower into depths where the punishment inflicted will continually detract more and more from the good which thou hast.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Thou exaltest thyself against God, if thou art indignant at His preceding thee; and thou art very contumacious in thy thoughts of Him, if thou dost not rejoice unspeakably in the possession of this good, that He alone is above thee.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: This being settled as certain, thou art not to say, God should have made me the only nature: there should be no good thing after me.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: It could not be that the next good thing to God should be the last.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And in this is seen most clearly how great dignity God conferred on thee, that He who in the order of nature alone rules over thee, made other good things for thee to rule over.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Nor be surprised that they are not now in all respects subject to thee, and that sometimes they pain thee; for thy Lord has greater authority over the things subject to thee than thou hast, as a master over the servants of his servants.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: What wonder, then, if, when thou sinnest, that is, disobeyest thy Lord, the things thou before ruledst over are made instrumental in thy punishment?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For what is so just, or what is more just than God?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For this befell human nature in Adam, of whom this is not the place to speak.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Suffice it to say, the righteous Ruler acts in character both in just rewards and in just punishments, in the happiness of those who live rightly, and in the penalty inflicted on sinners.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Nor yet art thou left without mercy, since by an appointed distribution of things and times thou art called to return.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Thus the righteous control of the supreme Creator extends even to earthly good things, which are corrupted and restored, that thou mightest have consolations mingled with punishments; that thou mightest both praise God when delighted by the order of good things, and mightest take refuge in Him when tried by experience of evils.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: So, as far as earthly things are subject to thee, they teach thee that thou art their ruler; as far as they distress thee, they teach thee to be subject to thy Lord.
